text: the single winning trade (cycle 34, +$37.94) entered UP at 0.35 fill price when BTC was already up $69 with only 65s remaining, suggesting late-candle momentum entries at discounted prices may have edge

both losing trades (cycles 25 and 26) bet DOWN during strong uptrend periods (BTC went up 6 consecutive candles from resolution 2-7), suggesting counter-trend DOWN bets during established UP momentum consistently failed

the two trades taken in this batch both occurred in cycles 97-98 on the same resolution, with a DOWN entry at 0.24 fill followed by an immediate SELL, resulting in a -$15.62 loss when BTC continued upward (+$140 move)

average PnL per traded resolution improved from $0.31 to $1.24 (+$0.93) despite maintaining 33% win rate, driven by the two winning trades in this batch having larger win sizes ($37.94 and $40.36) compared to the four losses averaging $17.73

both winning trades (cycles 87 and 91) were UP bets during a sustained uptrend period where BTC moved from $67,227 to $67,736 across 4 consecutive UP resolutions, with small price moves ($15 and $32 respectively)
all four losing trades across both batches were DOWN bets (cycles 25, 26, 88, 97), while both winning trades were UP bets, suggesting a directional bias where DOWN entries consistently underperformed
